step_by_step_reasoning: We trace the Snapchat architecture from the whiteboard and transcript. The client (iOS/Android) connects through an EKS Gateway (GW). The upload flow routes via EKS Media Service to CloudFront and S3. Metadata flow goes through EKS Messaging Service (MCS) and a custom SnapDB layer (running on EKS) to DynamoDB. The Friend Graph is another EKS microservice integrated into the stack. On the receive side, MCS queries ElastiCache for active connection/server IDs to quickly push the snap notification via GW, after which the client retrieves media from CloudFront/S3.

graph.name: Snapchat Massive Scale Media Delivery and Messaging
graph.notes: Handles 300M+ DAUs and 5B+ Snaps/day. Leverages a custom database layer (SnapDB) on EKS to manage transaction layers and ephemeral data before persisting metadata in DynamoDB. Connection states are cached in ElastiCache to enable fast, real-time message pushing.

nodes.name: Mobile Clients (iOS / Android)
nodes.notes: Hundreds of millions of daily active users sending and receiving snaps.

nodes.name: Gateway (GW)
nodes.notes: EKS-based API gateway managing user connections and API routing.

nodes.name: Media Service (MEDIA)
nodes.notes: EKS microservice responsible for media processing and delivery initiation.

nodes.name: Messaging Core Service (MCS)
nodes.notes: EKS microservice managing messaging logic and orchestrating recipient notification flows.

nodes.name: SnapDB
nodes.notes: Custom database layer built on EKS to handle transactions, TTL, and ephemeral data logic.

nodes.name: Friend Graph
nodes.notes: EKS microservice handling friend relationship mapping.

nodes.name: CloudFront
nodes.notes: Global CDN providing low-latency media caching and distribution.

nodes.name: S3
nodes.notes: Object storage storing the snap media payload.

nodes.name: ElastiCache
nodes.notes: Stores persistent connection mapping (server and connection IDs) for push routing.

nodes.name: DynamoDB
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: 400TB metadata storage. WORKLOAD_PEEK: Runs nightly scans processing 2B rows/minute for suggestions and cleaning ephemeral data.

edges.notes: Gateway routes upload request to Media Service.
Media is persisted in S3 close to the recipient.
Queries Friend Graph to validate messaging relations.
Gateway forwards message metadata to MCS.
Saves structured snap metadata to DynamoDB.
Queries cache for recipient's active connection and server ID.
Routes message notification payload to target gateway server.
Gateway pushes message notification to recipient client.
Retrieves snap image/video payload from S3.
